subject: Everything is down
body: None of our users can log in since 9am. This is affecting our entire company account. Please help immediately.
reasoning: The phrases "Everything is down," "None of our users can log in since 9am," and "affecting our entire company account" indicate a system-wide outage, which fits Technical / Bug with Critical urgency.

subject: Can't access the analytics dashboard I paid for
body: I upgraded to Pro yesterday specifically for the analytics dashboard but it just shows a blank white screen. I need this for a client call tomorrow.
reasoning: The phrases "analytics dashboard but it just shows a blank white screen" indicate a product malfunction, and "upgraded to Pro yesterday" plus "I need this for a client call tomorrow" shows a paid core feature the customer cannot access, making this High urgency.

subject: Export button broken
body: The CSV export button on the reports page doesn't work, but I can still copy-paste the data manually for now.
reasoning: The phrases "Export button broken" and "CSV export button on the reports page doesn't work" indicate a product malfunction, while "I can still copy-paste the data manually for now" shows a workaround exists, supporting Medium urgency.

subject: How do I change my invoice email?
body: Just wondering where in settings I can update the email address invoices get sent to.
reasoning: The phrases "invoice email" and "update the email address invoices get sent to" indicate a billing-related how-to question, which fits Billing & Payments with Low urgency.

subject: Would love dark mode
body: Any chance you're adding a dark mode option? Would make late-night work much easier on the eyes.
reasoning: The phrases "adding a dark mode option" and "would make late-night work much easier" clearly indicate a request for a new product feature rather than a bug or access issue.

subject: Concerned about my data
body: I think there may have been a data breach - I'm seeing login attempts from countries I've never visited. I'm considering involving a lawyer if this isn't addressed.
reasoning: The phrases "data breach" and "lawyer" indicate a security/legal escalation, while "login attempts from countries I've never visited" points to an account access concern.

subject: [Account tier: Enterprise] Question about SSO setup
body: We're trying to configure SSO for our team and the docs aren't quite matching what we see in the admin panel.
reasoning: The phrases "configure SSO for our team" and "docs aren't quite matching what we see in the admin panel" indicate an account access/setup issue, and the "[Account tier: Enterprise]" flag raises urgency to High under the enterprise floor rule.

subject: Billing issue?? or bug
body: I was charged twice but also the app crashed right after, not sure if related, maybe a bug caused a duplicate charge, please advise.
reasoning: The phrases "charged twice" suggest Billing & Payments while "app crashed" and "maybe a bug caused a duplicate charge" introduce Technical / Bug mixed signals, so the ticket is ambiguous and needs review.

subject: This is the third time I've had to email support
body: I've reported this same login bug twice before and nothing's been fixed. I'm extremely frustrated and considering canceling my subscription.
reasoning: The phrases "third time I've had to email support," "same login bug twice before," and "extremely frustrated and considering canceling my subscription" show both an unresolved complaint and an account-access/technical issue, so the best fit is Complaint / Escalation but the mixed signals require Human Review.

subject: Problem connecting earpods
